name: From Landscape between Seixas and Contim to Mezquita — driving through the Parque Natural de Montesinho
name_original: De Paisagem entre Seixas e Contim a Mezquita — condução pelo Parque Natural de Montesinho
region_intro: Road cycling around Santalha offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by its proximity to the Montesinho Natural Park and the Parque Biologico de Vinhais. The landscape features a mix of hills and valleys, providing a challenging environment for road cyclists. Routes often traverse natural settings, including areas with clean air and expansive views. The region's topography is well-suited for longer, more demanding rides. Best road cycling routes around Santalha The most popular road cycling route is Ring route from Vinhais, a difficult 38.1 miles (61.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the regional landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Vinhais and Montesinho-Nogueira Park Loop, a difficult 38.5 miles (62.0 km) path. This route offers extensive views within the Montesinho-Nogueira Park. Local road cyclists also love the Montesinho Natural Park and Vinhais Loop, a 60.4 miles (97.1 km) trail leading through Montesinho Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 37 minutes. Road cycling around Santalha is defined by its natural parks, rolling hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels, catering to experienced road cyclists. The routes in Santalha are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santalha's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Santalha?
A: Road cycling around Santalha offers a diverse landscape, characterized by its proximity to the Montesinho Natural Park and the Parque Biologico de Vinhais. You'll encounter a mix of rolling hills and valleys, providing a challenging yet rewarding environment for cyclists. The routes often traverse natural settings, offering clean air and expansive views.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Santalha?
A: The road cycling routes around Santalha are generally geared towards more experienced cyclists. The guide indicates that the available routes range from moderate to difficult ability levels, with a focus on longer and more demanding rides due to significant elevation gains.

Q: What are some popular road cycling routes in the Santalha region?
A: Among the local favorites is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2313664269/ring-route-from-vinhais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ring route from Vinhais&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 61.3 km trail. Another highly-rated option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399135310/vinhais-and-montesinho-nogueira-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinhais and Montesinho-Nogueira Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views within the park.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399116233/montesinho-natural-park-and-vinhais-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montesinho Natural Park and Vinhais Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 97.1 km trail that leads through the Montesinho Natural Park. Another challenging long route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1396557606/round-trip-to-ponte-hermisende-and-return-along-the-tuela-river-through-the-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Round trip to Ponte Hermisende and return along the Tuela River&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 83 km.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near significant natural areas. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4947686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biological Park of Vinhais&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4968010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hills in Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4945774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufeira de Prada&lt;/a&gt;. The region's natural parks are rich in biodiversity and offer picturesque backdrops for your rides.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit while road cycling?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can incorporate visits to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4989169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Vinhais&lt;/a&gt; into their rides. You might also encounter interesting sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7577400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Igrejinha Chapel&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4971054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moss-Covered Stone Ruins in Parque Natural de Montesinho&lt;/a&gt;, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Santalha?
A: The road cycling routes around Santalha are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Over 80 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and challenging aspects of the rides through the regional parks.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. Many routes provide extensive views within the Montesinho-Nogueira Park. Specifically, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4968010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Hills in Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6335556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Rabaçal river valley&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a break and photo opportunities.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Santalha area?
A: There are several road cycling routes detailed for the Santalha area, with a total of 5 distinct routes listed in this guide. These routes primarily focus on the challenging and scenic terrain of the natural parks.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the Montesinho Natural Park?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Santalha prominently feature the Montesinho Natural Park. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1399127028/from-landscape-between-seixas-and-contim-to-mezquita-driving-through-the-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;route from Seixas and Contim to Mezquita&lt;/a&gt; drives through the Parque Natural de Montesinho, offering immersive experiences within this protected area.

Q: Are there any routes that follow river valleys or pass by water bodies?
A: Yes, the region's landscape includes river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1396557606/round-trip-to-ponte-hermisende-and-return-along-the-tuela-river-through-the-parque-natural-de-montesinho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Round trip to Ponte Hermisende&lt;/a&gt; includes a return along the Tuela River, offering pleasant scenery alongside water. Additionally, the presence of river beaches like Praia fluvial of Segirei and Praia Fluvial da Ponte de Soeira suggests routes with potential views of water bodies.
tips: Spectacular views that appear in these mountains that unite the towns of Seixas and Contim, with mountains full of green forests and surrounded by nature.
Small town located in the extreme south of the country and that offers us beautiful views of its old church, San Pedro de A Mezquita. Also, the Pazo da Mezquita.
